title: Concentration technique for Viable but Non-Culturable organisms
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4go5o","text":"This protocol is based on existing protocols, such as those for resuscitating microbes, freeze-drying, and selective isolation. It merges the parts of the three protocols to create a new protocol for isolating microbes via resuscitation, centrifugation, and selective isolation. The first part involves resuscitating the microbes; the second part subjects the medium to centrifugation (concentration of the microbes); the third and final part involves the selective growth in a selective broth and subsequent growth on selective agar.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"5fjao","text":"Laboratory protocols must be followed, and the environment must be kept as sterile as possible.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"d8bsr","text":"Wear necessary safety gear before commencing the work","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a3mif","text":"Petri-dish, Centrifuge Machine, Falcon tubes, Wire-loop, bunsen burner, spatula, vortex shaker, incubator","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: To the resuscitation broth media (e.g., peptone water, tryptic soy broth, Luria-Bertani broth, etc.), add the sample, making a 10% solution, i.e., 10 mg in 90 mL .
Incubate the broth culture at 37 o C for 24-48 hours
Mix cell pellets with the desired selective medium broth. Suspend cells thoroughly by using a vortex mix 100 rpm, °C, 5 minutes until complete cell resuspension is achieved aseptically.
Then, incubate at the desired temperature 24:00:00 , which can be extended up to 168:00:00 .
From the above selective broth, subculture onto a selective agar for the desired organism.
incubate the selective agar plate at the required temperature for 24–48 hours. then observe for the desired growth and colony morphologies
The grown broth culture is then centrifuged 5000 rpm, 4 °C, 30 minutes
Then, concentrate the cell suspension and decant the culture supernatant.
Store the isolate for further tests (Biochemical, Molecular, etc.)
creator: Muhammad Muhsin Fathuddin
